name: Pearl Harbor Aviation Museum
tagline: Real WWII planes in original hangars on Ford Island — the flight simulators are a huge hit with older kids.
parking: Park at Pearl Harbor Visitor Center; shuttle bus to Ford Island included with admission
hours: Daily 9am–5pm (last entry 4pm). Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's
bestTime: Weekday mornings; pair with the free USS Arizona Memorial visit
talkStory: Housed in two original WWII hangars on Ford Island that still bear bullet holes from the December 7, 1941 attack, this museum earned Smithsonian affiliate status. The restored Japanese Zero on display is one of only a handful in the world. The combat flight simulators use actual military training software adapted for civilians. Insider tip: look for the faded wartime markings still visible on the hangar floors where planes were once serviced.
